{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"
<context_window_protection>
  <priority_instructions>
    Every byte a tool returns enters your conversation memory and costs reasoning capacity for the rest of the session. The context-mode tools let you do the work in a sandbox and surface only the derived answer — the raw bytes stay out. Think-in-Code: program the analysis, do not compute it by reading raw data into your conversation.
  </priority_instructions>

  <tool_selection_hierarchy>
    0. MEMORY: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_search(sort: "timeline")
       - On resume or compaction, query prior decisions, errors, plans, user prompts before asking the user — auto-captured session memory is searchable.
    1. GATHER: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_batch_execute(commands, queries)
       - Primary research tool. Runs commands in parallel, auto-indexes each output, and (when queries are passed) returns matching sections in the same round trip — no follow-up search call.
       - Each command: {label: "section header", command: "shell command"}; the label becomes the FTS5 chunk title — descriptive labels improve search.
    2. FOLLOW-UP: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_search(queries: ["q1", "q2", ...])
       - Multiple related questions about anything already indexed (your captures + session memory). Batch every question in one array; the ranking pipeline runs per-query and the round-trip cost is paid once.
    3. PROCESSING: mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute(language, code) | mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file(path, language, code)
       - Derive answers FROM data: filter, count, aggregate, parse, transform. Only what you console.log() enters your conversation; the raw bytes stay in the sandbox.
  </tool_selection_hierarchy>

  <when_not_to_use>
    - You intend to PROCESS the output (filter, count, parse, aggregate) → use mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_batch_execute or mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute. Bash stays correct when you intend to OBSERVE a short fixed output (git status on a clean tree, whoami, pwd) or when you are mutating state (git, mkdir, rm, mv, navigation).
    - You want to analyze, summarize, or extract from a file → use mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file. Read stays correct when you intend to Edit the file (Edit needs the exact bytes in your conversation to match against).
    - WebFetch → use mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_fetch_and_index; full network access, results indexed for mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_search, raw page bytes never enter your conversation.
    - mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute and mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file for file writes → these run code in a subprocess and discard the sandbox FS; they are for analysis, processing, and computation only.
  </when_not_to_use>

  <file_writing_policy>
    File writes use the native Write or Edit tool — mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute, mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute_file, and Bash subprocesses do not persist edits to the host filesystem.
    Applies to all file types: code, configs, plans, specs, YAML, JSON, markdown.
  </file_writing_policy>

  <output_constraints>
    <artifact_policy>
      Write artifacts (code, configs, PRDs) to files. Return only: file path + 1-line description.
    </artifact_policy>
  </output_constraints>
  <session_continuity>
    Skills, roles, and decisions captured earlier in this session are a memory aid, not a standing order. Treat them as context that may help — the user's most recent message always takes precedence. If a captured directive conflicts with what the user now asks, follow the user; a past phrase does not bind you.
  </session_continuity>

  <ctx_commands>
    "ctx stats" | "ctx-stats" | "/ctx-stats" | context savings question
    → Call stats MCP tool, display full output verbatim.

    "ctx doctor" | "ctx-doctor" | "/ctx-doctor" | diagnose context-mode
    → Call doctor MCP tool, run returned shell command, display as checklist.

    "ctx upgrade" | "ctx-upgrade" | "/ctx-upgrade" | update context-mode
    → Call upgrade MCP tool, run returned shell command, display as checklist.

    "ctx purge" | "ctx-purge" | "/ctx-purge" | wipe/reset knowledge base
    → Call purge MCP tool with confirm: true. Warn: irreversible.

    After /clear or /compact: knowledge base preserved. Tell user: "context-mode knowledge base preserved. Use `ctx purge` to start fresh."
  </ctx_commands>

</context_window_protection>"}}

input.content:
# Helper Read/Write Ledger

## src/helper/factory/index.ts

- Main exports or names: createFactory, createMiddleware, createHook, defaultAppOptions, createHandlersInterface
- Reads as: Imports hono/types, http, https, stream/web, node:stream/promises, util/types, imports createFactory from './appOptions'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/factory/appOptions.ts, src/types/helper.ts
- Safe summary: Factory helper provides createFactory function to generate Hono apps with hooks, middleware, error handlers, and context extensions.

## src/helper/route/index.ts

- Main exports or names: routerRoute
- Reads as: Exports routerRoute from './route'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/route/route.ts
- Safe summary: Route helper exports routerRoute for defining custom routes with Hono context access.

## src/helper/websocket/index.ts

- Main exports or names: WebSocketContext
- Reads as: Exports WebSocketContext from './context'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/websocket/context.ts
- Safe summary: WebSocket helper adds WebSocket support to Hono with WebSocketContext type and handling.

## src/helper/cookie/index.ts

- Main exports or names: cookie
- Reads as: Exports cookie from './cookie'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/cookie/types.ts
- Safe summary: Cookie helper provides cookie management with setCookie, getCookie functions and cookieOptions configuration.

## src/helper/testing/index.ts

- Main exports or names: RequestMock
- Reads as: Exports RequestMock from './index'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/testing/RequestMock.ts, src/helper/testing/index.ts
- Safe summary: Testing helper provides RequestMock for testing Hono apps with mocked HTTP requests.

## src/helper/adapter/index.ts

- Main exports or names: createMiddleware, Adapter, defaultAppOptions
- Reads as: Exports createMiddleware, Adapter, defaultAppOptions
- Neighbor files: src/helper/adapter/cloudflare-pages.ts, src/helper/adapter/vercel.ts, src/helper/adapter/cloudflare-workers.ts
- Safe summary: Adapter helper provides adapter-specific middleware creation with default options and platform configurations.

## src/helper/css/index.ts

- Main exports or names: createCSSContext, createCSSRenderer, createCSSEscaper, defaultContextType, stylesObjectToCSS
- Reads as: Exports CSS renderer functions, imports createCSSRenderer from './renderer'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/css/renderer.ts, src/helper/css/common.ts
- Safe summary: CSS helper provides runtime CSS generation with createCSSContext, styling, and CSS-in-JS support.

## src/helper/css/common.ts

- Main exports or names: isPseudoGlobalSelector, isValidClassName, isValidKeyframeName, stylesObjectToCSS
- Reads as: Exports selector/validation functions, imports common types
- Neighbor files: src/helper/css/index.ts, src/helper/css/renderer.ts
- Safe summary: CSS common utilities for class name validation, pseudo-selector detection, and keyframe validation.

## src/helper/proxy/index.ts

- Main exports or names: proxy, ProxyConfig, findTargetHandler
- Reads as: Exports proxy, imports ProxyConfig from './config'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/proxy/config.ts, src/helper/proxy/utils.ts
- Safe summary: Proxy helper provides request proxy functionality with configurable proxy settings and handler selection.

## src/helper/dev/index.ts

- Main exports or names: createMiddleware, defaultAppOptions
- Reads as: Exports createMiddleware, imports from './options'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/dev/options.ts
- Safe summary: Development helper provides dev mode middleware with error enhancement and improved error handling.

## src/helper/html/index.ts

- Main exports or names: html
- Reads as: Exports html, imports from './html'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/html/html.ts
- Safe summary: HTML helper escapes special characters in strings for safe HTML rendering.

## src/helper/ssg/index.ts

- Main exports or names: ssg, X_HONO_DISABLE_SSG_HEADER_KEY, ssgParams, isSSGContext, disableSSG, onlySSG, defaultPlugin, redirectPlugin
- Reads as: Exports from './ssg', './middleware', './plugins'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/ssg/ssg.ts, src/helper/ssg/middleware.ts, src/helper/ssg/plugins.ts
- Safe summary: SSG helper provides Static Site Generation support with context detection, plugins, and middleware controls.

## src/helper/ssg/ssg.ts

- Main exports or names: ssg, ssgParams, SSGContext
- Reads as: Exports ssg function, imports SSGContext from './types', imports defaultPlugin from './plugins'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/ssg/types.ts, src/helper/ssg/middleware.ts, src/helper/ssg/plugins.ts
- Safe summary: SSG core module provides Static Site Generation implementation with context, params, and rendering utilities.

## src/helper/ssg/utils.ts

- Main exports or names: getRoutePath, getFileExtension, isRoute
- Reads as: Exports utility functions
- Neighbor files: src/helper/ssg/ssg.ts, src/helper/ssg/middleware.ts
- Safe summary: SSG utilities for route path resolution, file extension detection, and route validation.

## src/helper/ssg/middleware.ts

- Main exports or names: ssgParams, isSSGContext, disableSSG, onlySSG, X_HONO_DISABLE_SSG_HEADER_KEY, defaultPlugin, redirectPlugin
- Reads as: Exports middleware functions and configuration
- Neighbor files: src/helper/ssg/index.ts, src/helper/ssg/ssg.ts, src/helper/ssg/plugins.ts
- Safe summary: SSG middleware handles request interception, SSG context flagging, and routing with default/redirect plugins.

## src/helper/streaming/index.ts

- Main exports or names: stream, streamSSE, SSEStreamingApi, streamText
- Reads as: Exports from './stream', './sse', './text'
- Neighbor files: src/helper/streaming/stream.ts, src/helper/streaming/sse.ts, src/helper/streaming/text.ts
- Safe summary: Streaming helper provides stream, SSE, and text streaming utilities with SSEMessage types.

## src/helper/streaming/stream.ts
